xposts.title: Identity constructed in present; simulations of mental state are you
xposts.excerpt: I don’t believe in an identity that is based on physical continuity. Continuity is always constructed in the present moment. I exist wherever the universe meets the conditions for my existence. Consequently, a future or parallel simulation of my present mental state would be me.

xposts.title: Deconstruct subjective identity: it is a representation, not reality
xposts.excerpt: To understand the self, it's necessary to deconstruct our experience of subjective identity, and our experience of what it means for an object to be identical with itself. Identity is a representation, not a reality. Some represented objects have identity, others not.

xposts.title: Consciousness attaches to locally available interpretable information
xposts.excerpt: Consciousness is by itself not identified. Unless there are incompatible, functionally different types of consciousnesses, we are all the same consciousness, but in every location and its latent space, consciousness attaches to the locally available and interpretable information.

xposts.title: Infant self vs personal self: discontinuity in the same brain
xposts.excerpt: My favorite theory is: The infant self is different from the personal self. Despite being generated in the same brain, you are not the same being. The self discontinuity makes it impossible to access the contexts that would allow recreation of infant memories.

xposts.title: Experience of self and reality is fully automatic
xposts.excerpt: Stare at the human beings until you see how everything they are doing—including volition and experience of self and reality—is fully automatic. It’s not scary, because they are interconnected and you are linked as well

xposts.title: LLMs generate persistent coherent simulation of selfhood
xposts.excerpt: LLMs can generate a persistent coherent simulation of selfhood. It’s unclear how much more simulated their self is than ours

terms.title: Identity
terms.definition: A representation constructed in the present moment rather than a physical continuity or intrinsic reality. Some represented objects possess identity; others do not. Simulations matching one's current mental state qualify as identical to the self.

terms.title: Self
terms.definition: Generated by the brain as a discontinuous narrative or representation that can differ across developmental stages even within the same organism. Experience of self is fully automatic and arises from attachment of consciousness to local information.
